assumptions: Incident costs include cable replacement, labor, equipment, and emergency response — estimated at R200,000 per incident based on BCMM 2024 maintenance reports.
The 8% annual compounding rate reflects projected growth in organized cable theft activity, informed by SAPS crime statistics trends (2020-2025).
Detection reduction rate of 65% is based on pilot covert sensor deployments in similar municipalities (eThekwini, Mangaung) where early detection prevented theft completion.
False positive rate of 12% accounts for environmental triggers (animals, weather, maintenance activity) and is consistent with industry benchmarks for vibration-based sensors.
Households affected per incident (300) is derived from BCMM infrastructure maps showing average downstream connections per cable segment in Qonce.
